headline: Don't underestimate the role of Palantir, AI in Iran conflict
summary: Defense stocks — like Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC) — are sliding in Tuesday's session amid escalations in the Middle East conflict after Israel bombed Iran and Lebanon. Baird Tech Strategist Ted Mortonson comes on Market Domination to discuss the role Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and other AI operators could have in Iran. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

headline: Defense companies poised to ramp up production amid Iran war
summary: Israel's airstrikes against Iran and Lebanon have Wall Street investors anxious over the risk these escalations could have on global markets if they continue for too long, as not only US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are seeing losses in Tuesday's session, but the defense industry as well. Gabelli Funds portfolio manager Tony Bancroft comes on Market Catalysts to discuss the defense manufacturer's inventory-production dynamic as Middle East conflicts ramp up and how he is forecasting increased capex spending for these contractors. Bancroft manages the Gabelli Commercial Aerospace and Defense ETF (GCAD). To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Iran, Dip-Buyers & a Rare Buffett Stumble: Wall Street Shakes Off Its Worst Fears

headline: Northrop Grumman (NOC) Is Up 5.9% After Record Backlog and 2026 Guidance Update - What's Changed
summary: In late January 2026, Northrop Grumman reported higher fourth-quarter 2025 adjusted earnings and sales across all major segments, a record backlog of about US$95.68 billion, and issued 2026 guidance calling for revenues of US$43.5–US$44.0 billion and adjusted earnings of US$27.40–US$27.90 per share. At the same time, the company’s heavy investment in Digital Engineering and progress on programs such as the B-21 Raider and new satellite contracts underscored how advanced development pipelines...

headline: Northrop Grumman Backlog And B 21 Progress Under Geopolitical Tailwinds
summary: Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) is benefiting from rising geopolitical tensions following joint U.S. and Israeli actions in Iran. The company has reported a record backlog and solid fourth quarter results across all operating segments. Management is advancing key programs, including accelerated production of the B-21 Raider stealth bomber and increased investment in digital engineering capabilities. For investors tracking defense names, Northrop Grumman sits at the center of several big themes,...
